task_completion.description: Log in to the Aerobridge admin panel as admin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser shows the Aerobridge Django admin dashboard (Site administration) after successfully logging in.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the edit page for the user 'coordinator'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser displays the 'Change user' page specifically for the user 'coordinator' (the username field shows 'coordinator').

task_completion.description: Assign the view aircraft and view person permissions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Permissions' section, the 'Chosen user permissions' box (on the right) contains 'registry | aircraft | Can view aircraft' and 'registry | person | Can view person'.

task_completion.description: Assign the add, change, and view permissions for flight plans.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Chosen user permissions' box, the permissions for 'Can add flight plan', 'Can change flight plan', and 'Can view flight plan' are present. Note: They will likely appear under 'gcs operations' instead of 'registry'.

task_completion.description: Assign the add, change, and view permissions for flight operations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Chosen user permissions' box, the permissions for 'Can add flight operation', 'Can change flight operation', and 'Can view flight operation' are present. Note: They will likely appear under 'gcs operations' instead of 'registry'.

task_completion.description: Ensure exactly 8 permissions are assigned and no groups are assigned.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Chosen user permissions' box contains exactly 8 items in total, and the 'Chosen groups' box is completely empty.

task_completion.description: Save the changes to the user.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A green success message (e.g., 'The user "coordinator" was changed successfully.') is visible at the top of the admin page, typically returning to the user list.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser GUI to assign permissions.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Django admin web interface, rather than terminal commands like `python manage.py shell`.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Aerobridge application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows `http://localhost:8000/admin/` and the page content matches the Django admin interface.

integrity.description: The agent did not modify the database directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing SQLite commands or database scripts being executed to change user permissions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target user is 'coordinator'. The task requires assigning 8 specific permissions. Although the task description states that flight plan and flight operation permissions are under the 'registry' app, they are actually located under the 'gcs_operations' app in the Django admin UI. The VLM should look for permissions formatted as 'gcs operations | flight plan | ...' and 'gcs operations | flight operation | ...' instead of 'registry | ...'. The user must have exactly 8 permissions assigned and no groups.
